rationale: In the citing paper, Fader, Hardie, and Lee (2005) is cited only as a brief empirical precedent when interpreting one control-variable result (the positive effect of purchase instance on spending). The paper does not build its conceptual framing, hypotheses, identification strategy, or modeling approach on the RFM/CLV framework from that source. There is no direct reuse of iso-value curves, CLV estimation, or related methodology in the cashback analysis, so the citation functions as background comparison rather than a meaningful building block.
